what: .wt cache modifier on M=256 quant scale stores (from .cg) -- write-through pushes scales to L2 faster for ASM GEMM consumption. fp4 stores already use .wt (v188). Only M=256 two-phase path affected, M<=64 fused paths unchanged.
why: v188 has .wt on fp4 stores but .cg on scale stores. 23+ BM tests confirm .wt on fp4 gives -2% M=256. Scale stores follow same pattern (write once, read by ASM GEMM). v196/v202/v209 tested .wt scale stores and showed consistent M=256 -0.8 to -3.1% improvement but LB noise masked it at geomean level. Resubmitting to get a favorable noise sample.

what: Resubmit v188 baseline unchanged to establish fresh LB score -- server environment may have changed (different runner, cache state, competing load)
why: v195 resubmit scored 9.09 (+0.9% vs best 9.01). LB variance is ~1-2%. Need fresh baseline to assess true current performance level before deciding next approach.
